message:
refactor(cancel): migrate is_cancel_requested() to checkpoint().is_err()

Replace every call-site of the legacy `is_cancel_requested()` polling
API with the structured `checkpoint().is_err()` pattern across the
entire codebase. The checkpoint API records cancellation observations in
the scope tree for diagnostics and correctly handles cascading cancel
propagation, whereas the old boolean flag only checked a single atomic
without updating scope state.

Affected subsystems:

- database/mysql: connect, query, execute, ping, and row-streaming loops
- database/postgres: connect, query, execute, ping paths
- database/pool: sleep-wait cancellation polling and pool acquire
- database/sqlite: connect and query paths
- actor: message loop, stopping detection, context helpers
- combinator/retry: inner-future cancellation detection
- http/h1 client and server: request/response cancellation checks
- net/websocket client, server, split: send, recv, and read_some_io
- service/service: poll_ready cancellation
- web/request_region: region deadline cancellation

Also adds `#[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)]` to the datagram cancel test
that tripped the clippy lint after recent expansion.

No behavioural change — all paths already returned cancellation errors;
this unifies the mechanism for correctness and observability.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
